own_review.issue: IndexOutOfBoundsException if ConfigurationParams.getItems() returns an empty list; no size/bounds guard before .get(0)
getScopeUri() may return null; new URI(null) throws NullPointerException, NOT URISyntaxException, so it escapes the catch(URISyntaxException) and crashes configuration()

If URISyntaxException is caught (or getParentFile() returns null for a root URI), highestPackageJsonDir stays null and is dereferenced -> NPE. The catch only holds a comment 'shouldn't happen else what to do here?'
Unbounded walk up to filesystem root selecting the HIGHEST package.json; can climb to '/', pick up an unintended ancestor package.json, and incur filesystem stat cost per level
Boolean config values passed as String "true" instead of JSON boolean true; ESLint LSP likely expects a boolean
Capacity 6 with load factor 1.0 and 6 entries will trigger a resize; minor sizing nit (human reviewer flagged the related sizing comment)

points.note: Matches own finding #1; real, no bounds check

points.note: Correctly identifies uncaught NPE blocker (own finding #2)

Accurate context note, no issue raised

exceeds: Missing-error-logging point with verified codebase convention citations (beyond human reference)

points.loc: ESLintClientImpl.java:51-52 critical null-safety bug, null after URISyntaxException catch -> NPE on line 54/60
points.note: Real blocker (own finding #3); quotes the 'shouldn't happen' comment, suggests guard/fallback
Real behavior change (executable path -> package.json dir); well-reasoned, asks if validated. Not in human reference

missed: Unbounded package.json walk to filesystem root (own finding #4)
String-typed booleans "true" in codeAction map (own finding #5 / human lines 72-73)
exceeds: nodePath semantic/behavior-change analysis tying client config to ESLintLanguageServer launch (beyond human reference)

qualitative: Both reviews correctly land the core blocker: the post-catch null dereference of highestPackageJsonDir leading to an NPE in configuration(). v7 is broader and more accurate on null-safety, additionally catching the IndexOutOfBoundsException on getItems().get(0) and the distinct uncaught new URI(null) NPE, plus a well-substantiated missing-error-logging point whose cited convention files I verified exist. v8 is tighter and contributes a genuinely useful nodePath behavior-change analysis that neither v7 nor the human raised, but it misses the IOOBE and is otherwise thinner. Both miss the unbounded walk to the filesystem root, the string-typed boolean values ("true" where the ESLint LSP expects a JSON boolean), and the HashMap sizing nit the human flagged. Neither produced any absence-fabrications; v8 being a Docker rebuild of v7 shows the same reasoning shape but with reduced coverage on this PR.
